claim_text: All six strict transform families are state-transition-equivalent (byte-identical post-call snapshots over >15000 property cases, zero mismatch).
Enum-encoding causes a small paired success drop for both models (Claude -0.20, Codex -0.10).
statistical_support: directional, NOT significant: both bootstrap CIs include 0 (Claude [-0.50,0], Codex [-0.30,0]); n=10 single-seed; 1.0 ceiling

claim_text: Degradation persists after matching schema tokens / content (not explained by length).
reviewer_attack: effect vanishes under content match -> claim fails

claim_text: Model ranking reverses across equivalent interfaces (Kendall tau < 1).
statistical_support: NOT supported: <=0.10 enum gap within bootstrap noise; needs >=6 model families + Kendall-tau
limitations: 2 models cannot support a ranking claim

claim_text: A label-free STNF reduces degradation on held-out transforms vs the strongest non-oracle baseline.
limitations: PRIMARY claim, UNRUN -> pre-registered plan; static canonicalizer provably abstains on enum
reviewer_attack: STNF just reverts to original interface
